name: Hauula
desc: Small windward coast village — stunningly green, very quiet, tight-knit community, long commute but gorgeous scenery.
insider: Uncle Bobo's has the best smoked meat plate on the windward coast — the kalua pork is worth the drive alone
Hauula Loop Trail is a moderate 2.5-mile hike through Norfolk pines with incredible ridge views and almost zero crowds
Hauula Beach Park has camping with a city permit — one of the prettiest and most peaceful camp spots on Oahu
Cell service drops to near-zero in stretches between Hauula and Kahuku — download maps and music before you arrive
The commute to Honolulu is 45-60 minutes minimum and there is only one road — Kamehameha Highway, no shortcuts
Sacred Falls State Park has been closed since a 1999 rockslide killed hikers — do not attempt to enter, it is illegal and dangerous
Papa Ole's Kitchen serves solid Hawaiian plates when it's open — hours can be unpredictable so call ahead first
The tiny post office and general store area is the entire commercial district — drive to Laie or Kaneohe for real shopping
Housing is among the most affordable on the windward coast, but flood insurance requirements can add to the real cost
This stretch of coast was the backdrop for scenes in Jurassic Park and 50 First Dates — the mountain scenery is truly cinematic
